note: Versioned model-dialect table for the Studio S prompt compiler. Each profile encodes how to phrase prompts for that model, what it's good/bad at, and routing rules. Update this FILE when models change — never hardcode dialect rules in app code. Show 'tunedFor' + 'profilesDate' in the Review tab so users know how fresh the advice is. Sources: 2026-07-01 research sweep (see FILMMAKER-REVIEW-2026-07-01.md digest).

stillModels.label: GPT Image 2

do: Full descriptive sentences
World-knowledge references (real places, eras, brands where cleared)
dont: Keyword soup
Flag syntax

strengths: Currently tops several preference leaderboards (LMArena, Artificial Analysis)

stillModels.label: Nano Banana Pro

dialect.structure: Image / Content / Style brief — it's a reasoning model, write a structured brief, not keywords.
do: Attach reference frames for character consistency
Put exact tagline/signage text in quotes — best in-image text of any model
Use for multi-person scenes (holds up to 5 people)
dont: Expect moody/aesthetic film looks — outputs can read 'technically correct'; pair with a strong style block

strengths: Best character consistency (up to 14 reference images, 5 consistent people)
Best in-image text rendering (taglines, signage, multi-language)
Reasoning + search grounding for factual accuracy
weaknesses: Aesthetics/mood weaker than FLUX.2 — 'generated by software rather than shot by a photographer'

references.shape: up to 14 reference images; up to 5 consistent people

stillModels.versionWarning: Nano Banana 2 (Gemini 3.1 Flash Image, Feb 2026) is a SPEED tier, not an upgrade — don't switch to it for hero frames.

dialect.structure: Concise shot description; lean on material fidelity words (glass, asphalt, fabric).
do: Multi-reference composition (up to ~10 frames) to lock brand look

stillModels.label: FLUX.2

do: Natural skin/texture language
dont: Sharpness keywords — FLUX over-crisps on its own

weaknesses: Generation-to-generation consistency DRIFT: faces/clothing/backgrounds shift between runs — practitioner-confirmed, contradicts BFL marketing

stillModels.label: Midjourney v7

dialect.structure: Keyword phrases + film-stock color science + flags.
do: Film stocks (Kodak Portra 400) for color science
Use --oref (Omni Reference) for character/object identity — NOT --cref, which errors in v7
--cw/--ow weight 0-100 to tune identity strength
dont: Expect literal prompt accuracy — aesthetic interpretation wins

weaknesses: Identity holds best on clean frontal reference images only

dialect.structure: Straight text-to-image; strong text rendering; no editing/reference features.

bestFor: moodboard/exploration passes before spending on hero models

videoModels.label: Kling

dialect.structure: Camera instruction FIRST; the still is an anchor. Subject action, then scene, then camera. Preserve-framing language ('preserve the source framing and signage').
do: One primary camera move
Keep environmental events minimal — Kling spends its budget on subject + camera
Use dialogue/sound lines: 2.6+ generates synced speech, singing, SFX, ambient natively (CN+EN)
dont: Trust long takes — temporal consistency is Kling's known weakness (abrupt location changes, blurry faces); regenerate rather than nudge
Combine Motion Brush or End Frame with camera movement — Kling DISABLES camera moves when either is present (enforce in UI)

strengths: Motion realism leader — best for final/branded motion-heavy shots
Deepest reference API: 10-image multi-reference, Bind Subject (face+clothing lock in i2v), multi-fusion (character + environment + style from separate images), Elements (1-4 images)
Motion Brush: up to 6 elements with independent trajectories + Static Brush

references.shape: multi-image reference / Elements (1-4) / Bind Subject / video reference

videoModels.label: Seedance 2.0

dialect.structure: Director-level camera language now works: dolly zoom, rack focus, tracking, POV switches, handheld. Sequenced moves tolerated (unlike most models). Rhythmic pacing words still help.
do: Use multi-shot cuts inside one 4-15s generation for connected beats
Write dialogue — lip-synced speech is native and free
Regenerate rather than nudge — small prompt tweaks cause large framing shifts
dont: Close-up hands (weak)
On-screen text (unreliable — route text to a Nano Banana Pro still and composite in Finishing)
Expect subtle micro-expressions

strengths: Native lip-sync at no extra cost
weaknesses: Coarse fine-control — 'almost right' needs full regen

continuity.notes: multi-shot cuts internal to a generation

videoModels.versionWarning: 1.0-era doctrine ('one camera move, rhythmic language only') is OBSOLETE — 2.0 rewrote the dialect.

videoModels.label: Runway Gen-4.5

dialect.structure: Concise motion brief. Emphasize 'stable / consistent / no distortion' to avoid drift.
do: Single-image character consistency via References (up to 3 images)
Use for prompt-faithful tests — tops the Elo leaderboard for adherence
Multi-shot long-form (up to ~1 min) with character consistency post-Dec-2025
dont: Deliver as final 1080p+ master — 720p ceiling
Assume old 'Runway is silent' advice — native audio since Dec 11, 2025

strengths: Aleph: targeted video-to-video edits — change one element WITHOUT regenerating (use for the fix-don't-reroll path)
weaknesses: Character drift in complex multi-shot work

dialect.structure: Narrative phrasing — describe a short filmed scene, not parameters. Structured prompts improve probabilistic audio sync.
do: Physics-forward action (rebounds, weight)
Cameos for consented likeness (app-only, not API)
Storyboards feature for multi-clip planning
dont: Fast pans/spins (break)
Many characters
Precise spatial placement — OpenAI's own system card admits left/right confusion and failed camera trajectories

audio.note: sync is probabilistic — review pass required

specs.resolution: up to 1080p (unofficial — OpenAI publishes no spec table)

videoModels.label: Veo 3.1

dialect.structure: Explicit camera language required — unspecified camera DEFAULTS TO STATIC (Google's own guidance). One primary move; stacked moves in a short clip produce 'confused' output.
do: Dialogue lines — best-validated native audio (48kHz, ~120ms lip-sync)
Ingredients to Video: up to 3 reference images for character/style
First & Last Frame for match cuts and transitions
Scene extension for 60s+ (conditions on final second of prior clip — drift compounds per join, test each extension in motion)
dont: Short speech segments (Google's acknowledged soft spot)
Long/complex shots — fine detail and continuity degrade
Stacked camera moves

specs.extendedVia: scene extension (60s+ is stitching, not one take)

strengths: Richest control toolkit (Ingredients, First & Last Frame, style ref, object add/remove)
weaknesses: Movement can feel stiff vs Kling

continuity.notes: the primary compile target for 'open on closing pose of shot X'

videoModels.rosterNote: NOT currently in the Studio S roster — add it. Two of the top grievance-solvers (native audio + first/last frame) ship here.

videoModels.label: Luma Ray3.14

dialect.structure: Composable natural-language camera moves ('orbit left + crane up + push in') — Camera Motion Concepts, 15+ types.
do: Up to 16 keyframes per clip
Ray3 Modify for reference-driven video-to-video that preserves performance/timing
16-bit HDR pipeline for grade-heavy finishing
dont: Expect audio — silent, add in post
Use Ray3.14 for characters — it DROPS character reference; fall back to Ray 3
Trust identity without re-anchoring — Luma's own docs say re-anchor to a Master Reference every generation

strengths: HDR color pipeline (only native 16-bit HDR model)

references.shape: Character Reference (Ray 3 only, up to 4 images of same person)

videoModels.label: Midjourney Video V1

dialect.structure: Animate an existing MJ still; low/high motion slider.
dont: Use for client deliverables — 480p base / 720p max, silent, image-animation only

weaknesses: Lowest resolution of any major model

globalLintRules.warn: Complex hand shots succeed only 60-75% across all models. Reframe, or budget extra takes.

globalLintRules.trigger: signage/tagline/on-screen text in a video prompt
globalLintRules.warn: In-video text is gibberish-prone everywhere. Route the text to a Nano Banana Pro still and composite in Finishing.
One primary camera move per short clip (Google's own Veo guidance; Sora reviewers agree). Exception: Seedance 2.0 tolerates sequenced moves.

globalLintRules.trigger: placement-grid position compiled as text ('subject on right third')
globalLintRules.warn: Models confuse left/right (OpenAI system card). Compile placement via the composed still / first-frame anchor, not prose.

globalLintRules.trigger: choreography names some but not all subjects
globalLintRules.warn: Precisely-directed subjects move correctly; everything unmentioned freezes (precision-vs-dynamism tradeoff). Name every mover.

globalLintRules.trigger: 3 takes on a shot with no keeper
globalLintRules.warn: Change one variable before rolling again (~80% of credits die on rerolls). Single-variable testing.

globalLintRules.trigger: small change requested on a near-keeper take
globalLintRules.warn: Route to a targeted-edit tool (Runway Aleph, Luma Modify) instead of full regeneration.

globalLintRules.trigger: FLUX.2 selected for a shot referencing a locked character
globalLintRules.warn: FLUX.2 drifts between generations — use Nano Banana Pro for recurring characters.

globalLintRules.trigger: dialogue/sound field non-empty + Luma or Midjourney Video selected
globalLintRules.warn: This model is silent. Route to Kling 2.6+, Seedance 2.0, Veo 3.1, Sora 2, or Runway Gen-4.5 — or plan audio in post.

provenanceCapture.note: Capture per take, for the human-authorship manifest (US Copyright Office: only documented human contributions are protectable; vendor C2PA/SynthID strips in the NLE pipeline).
